meta.title: 1💗1 · Grow Together — a living from the land, without going it alone
meta.description: An ecological livelihood that starts from community, not from risk. Land, seedlings, hands and time — everyone brings what they have and love, and together you grow a harmonious, circular living. Start from the customers and build the business around the need.

hero.eyebrow: ◈ Ecological work · Tending the land · Community ◈
hero.h1: A living from the land — without doing it alone
hero.h2: An ecological profession starts from community, not from risk
hero.h3: Everyone brings what they have and love — together a livelihood grows
hero.h4: From the eater back to the soil — one whole, harmonious circle
hero.h5: Start from the customers. The business is built around the need
hero.sub: You don't need land, capital, or to start alone. Each person brings a piece — soil, seedlings, hands, time or customers — and the system fairly records who gave what, and shares the harvest.
hero.ctaSecondary: I want a local veggie box 🥬

problem.title: Starting a growing business alone — nearly impossible
problem.lead: The dream is simple: work the land, grow real food, earn a dignified living. It's the barriers that stop you.
problem.b1: You have no land — and those who do are afraid to hand it to strangers.
problem.b2: No capital for seedlings, tools and a greenhouse before you've earned a cent.
problem.b3: No guaranteed customers — and half the produce rots or goes to waste.
problem.b4: Alone from dawn to dusk, no day of rest and no one to relieve you.
problem.cost: So the dream stays a dream, and the land stays fallow.
problem.turn: At 1💗1 every barrier becomes someone else's role ✿

weave.title: A weave: everyone brings a thread, a whole cloth emerges
weave.sub: Not a partnership where everyone does everything. Each person joins with what they have and love — and the system remembers every hour and resource, so the split stays fair.
weave.r1_d: Provides the plot, knows they'll profit from it, and is involved in every decision. The land works — instead of lying fallow.
weave.r2_d: Enjoys growing seedlings in quantity, and is happy to contribute them as an investment — and to be involved in how things grow.
weave.r3_d: Comes from morning to evening for the love of it. It's not a 'job', it's a passion — and it's counted as hours put into the system.
weave.r4_d: When the grower wants a day of rest, someone steps in. No one is chained to the land alone, 365 days a year.
weave.r5_d: Everyone puts in hours and resources — and the system records who gave what, sharing the harvest and the money by real contribution.
weave.note: No single 'owner' and 'employees'. A network of givers — each with a transparent, measurable share.

demand.title: Start from the customer — not from the risk
demand.sub: Instead of growing and hoping someone buys, start from people who already want. The risk of 'no customers and produce in the bin' simply disappears.
demand.p1: People in a given area ask for a weekly veggie box — that's the demand.
demand.p2: Around them, the beginning grower organizes with the landholder, the seedling grower and the carers.
demand.p3: You grow exactly what was ordered, in the quantity ordered — minimum waste, maximum freshness.
demand.basket: 🥬 A weekly veggie box, local and fresh — built around the people who actually want it.

paths.title: Two ways — whichever fits you
paths.simple_d: Grow, sell, and the system simply splits the money among everyone who contributed — hours, land, seedlings. No accounting headaches.
paths.economy_t: A full economy around the customer
paths.economy_d: A personal concierge: the customer says what they want, and a group forms around the need — a weekly box, a subscription, complementary products. A living local economy.
paths.note: Start small with simple sales, and grow into a full economy — at your own pace.

how.title: How to start — 4 steps
how.s1_t: Join and say what you have
how.s1_d: Land? Seedlings? Time and hands? Or are you a customer who wants a local box? Every starting point is good.
how.s2_d: The system connects you — landholder, grower, carer and customers — by area, need and what each person loves to do.
how.s3_d: Who gives what, who gets what, and how it's split. All visible, measurable, and approved by everyone before you begin.
how.s4_d: The land works, the box reaches the customer, and every hour and resource translates into a fair share of fruit and money.

who.title: Who it's for
who.sub: For anyone with a piece to give — and anyone who wants to eat better.
who.w1_d: With a free plot that could work and yield — through involvement, not surrender.
who.w2_d: Who love the soil and want that love to become a livelihood too.
who.w3_d: Who want to start without huge capital and without the risk of going it alone.
who.w4_d: Looking to thicken a local, circular, shared economy around the land.
who.w5_d: Who want a fresh, local veggie box — and to know the hands that grew it.

values.title: Harmony, circularity, and a real connection
values.v1_d: From seed to plate and back to the soil. No waste, no needless middlemen — a closed loop between giver and eater.
values.v2_d: Everyone does what they love and are good at. Livelihood is born from joy, not from burnout.
values.v3_d: Every hour, every seedling and every coin — recorded and visible. Trust is built on fair measurement, not promises.

cta.title: The land is waiting. The community is ready.
cta.sub: Whether you have a plot, hands, seedlings — or just a hunger for real food — there's a place for you in the circle.
